test_cases.test_case: Successful teacher login redirects to Dashboard
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page; valid <teacher credentials> are available
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the 'Log in' button
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Dashboard; the Dashboard page is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Access site as a guest to browse limited content
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: Limited unauthenticated browsing of site content is granted and guest-accessible content is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Open Cookies notice to view cookie usage information
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page
steps: 1. Click the 'Cookies notice' button
test_cases.expected_result: A dialog or panel opens displaying cookie usage information

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Username blank (required text)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Enter <valid password> in the Password field
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Username field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the login page and is not authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Submit with Password blank (required password)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the login page
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Password field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; user remains on the login page and is not authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Submit with both Username and Password empty
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field blank
2. Leave the Password field blank
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation errors appear on both Username and Password fields indicating they are required; the form does not submit; user remains on the login page and is not authenticated

test_cases.test_case: Submit with invalid credentials (wrong password)
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the login page
A valid Username exists to test incorrect authentication
steps: 1. Enter <valid username> in the Username field
2. Enter <invalid password> in the Password field
3. Click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Inline authentication error appears on the form indicating sign-in failed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains its value; the form does not submit and the user remains unauthenticated (no redirect to Dashboard)

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Lost password?' when the link is disabled on this test site
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and on the login page
The 'Lost password?' link is marked disabled on this test site (per spec)
steps: 1. Locate the 'Lost password?' link on the login form
2. Click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Lost password?' link is disabled/unresponsive (not navigable); no password-reset page or flow opens; no navigation occurs and the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access the login page / perform Log in while already authenticated (precondition violation)
preconditions: User is already authenticated and has an active session
steps: 1. With an authenticated session, navigate to the site's login page
2. Attempt to view or interact with the Login form or click the "Log in" button
test_cases.expected_result: Access to the login form is blocked for authenticated users: the system redirects the user away from the login page to the Dashboard (or the Login form is not visible); the Log in action is not available and no re-authentication occurs

test_cases.test_case: Attempt 'Access as a guest' while authenticated (precondition violation)
preconditions: User is already authenticated and has an active session
steps: 1. With an authenticated session, navigate to the page containing the 'Access as a guest' control
2. Attempt to click the 'Access as a guest' button
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Access as a guest' action is not available to authenticated users: the button is either not visible or is disabled; clicking it does not grant limited unauthenticated browsing and the user's authenticated session remains active

test_cases.test_case: Username with leading and trailing whitespace causes credential mismatch
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page
Valid credentials (username without extra whitespace and matching password) are available
steps: 1. Enter a username with leading and trailing whitespace in the Username field
2. Enter the valid matching password in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked / error shown: an inline error message is displayed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered value including leading/trailing whitespace as shown on the page

test_cases.test_case: Username and Password containing emoji / non-ASCII characters
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page
No account in the system contains the exact emoji/non-ASCII-padded credentials
steps: 1. Enter a Username containing emoji and other non-ASCII characters in the Username field
2. Enter a Password containing emoji and other non-ASCII characters in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button
test_cases.expected_result: Login is blocked / error shown: an inline error message is displayed; the Password field is cleared; the Username field retains the entered emoji/non-ASCII characters as shown on the page

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-submit of Log in button with valid credentials
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page
Valid credentials are available for successful sign-in
steps: 1. Enter the valid Username in the Username field
2. Enter the valid Password in the Password field
3. Click the Log in button twice in rapid succession
test_cases.expected_result: Form submits successfully; user is redirected to the Dashboard once; the second rapid click is ignored (no additional navigation or duplicate side effects occur)

test_cases.test_case: Clicking disabled 'Lost password?' link is ignored
preconditions: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page
'Lost password?' link is disabled on this test site
steps: 1. Click the 'Lost password?' link
test_cases.expected_result: Click is blocked / no navigation: no password reset UI or navigation appears; the page remains on the login screen and the 'Lost password?' control remains visually disabled

test_cases.test_case: Select 'Next 7 days' time range when activities exist
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
User has timeline activities scheduled within the next 7 days
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Timeline block, open the Time Range dropdown and select 'Next 7 days'
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline refreshes to show items within the selected range; if no items exist within the selected range, an empty state is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Select 'Next 7 days' time range when no activities exist (empty state)
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
User has no timeline activities scheduled within the next 7 days
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Timeline block, open the Time Range dropdown and select 'Next 7 days'
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline refreshes to show items within the selected range; if no items exist within the selected range, an empty state is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Change timeline sort order and verify reorder
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
Timeline contains multiple activities across the selected time range
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Timeline block, open the Sort Order dropdown and select <sort order option>
3. Wait for the Timeline to refresh/reorder
test_cases.expected_result: Timeline is reordered according to the selected sort order; activities appear in the new order in the Timeline block.

test_cases.test_case: Search timeline with matching query shows filtered results
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
Timeline contains at least one activity whose name or type matches <matching query>
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Timeline block, enter <matching query> into the Search field and submit/search
test_cases.expected_result: Search results are shown in the timeline; the Timeline displays only activities that match <matching query> and unrelated items are no longer visible.

test_cases.test_case: Search timeline with no matching query shows empty state
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
No timeline activity matches <non-matching query>
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Timeline block, enter <non-matching query> into the Search field and submit/search
test_cases.expected_result: Search results are shown in the timeline; if no items match the query, an empty-state is shown.

test_cases.test_case: Filter calendar by a specific course
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
There are calendar events from multiple courses in the visible month
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Calendar block, open the 'All courses' dropdown and select <course>
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar events are filtered to the selected course; the month view shows only event names and dates belonging to <course>, and events from other courses are not displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to previous month in calendar
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
Calendar is displaying the current month
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Previous month' button
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to the previous month; event dates and current-date highlighting refresh to reflect the new month.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to next month in calendar
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
Calendar is displaying the current month
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Next month' button
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to the next month; event dates and current-date highlighting refresh to reflect the new month.

test_cases.test_case: Click 'New event' opens the calendar event creation flow
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'New event' button
test_cases.expected_result: opens calendar event creation flow

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Full calendar' navigates to the dedicated calendar view
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Full calendar' link
test_cases.expected_result: navigates to dedicated calendar view

test_cases.test_case: Open 'Import or export calendars' management
preconditions: User logged in as <Authenticated user>
Dashboard page is reachable from top navigation
steps: 1. Navigate to Dashboard via the top navigation 'Dashboard' link
2. In the Calendar block, click the 'Import or export calendars' link
test_cases.expected_result: opens calendar data management

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access Dashboard page
preconditions: User is logged out (unauthenticated)
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the Dashboard link in the top navigation bar or open the Dashboard URL
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Login page and sees the Login form; the Dashboard greeting and the Timeline and Calendar blocks are not displayed (Dashboard content is blocked).

test_cases.test_case: Clicking 'New event' is blocked when user is not logged in
preconditions: User is logged out (unauthenticated)
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the Dashboard (or the page containing the 'New event' control).
2. Click the 'New event' button in the Calendar block.
test_cases.expected_result: The New event action is blocked: the calendar event creation flow does not open; the user is redirected to the Login page (Login form is shown) or a login prompt appears. No event creation dialog is displayed.

test_cases.test_case: Access to 'Full calendar' view is blocked when user is not logged in
preconditions: User is logged out (unauthenticated)
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the Dashboard (or the page containing the 'Full calendar' link).
2. Click the 'Full calendar' link.
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the dedicated calendar view is blocked: the user is redirected to the Login page and sees the Login form; the dedicated calendar view is not opened.

test_cases.test_case: Access to 'Import or export calendars' is blocked when user is not logged in
preconditions: User is logged out (unauthenticated)
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user, navigate to the Dashboard (or the page containing the 'Import or export calendars' link).
2. Click the 'Import or export calendars' link.
test_cases.expected_result: Access to calendar data management is blocked: the user is redirected to the Login page and sees the Login form; the import/export management page is not opened.

test_cases.test_case: Very-long search query (200+ characters) in Timeline Search
preconditions: User is authenticated and Dashboard is open
Timeline block is visible
steps: 1. Click the Timeline Search field
2. Enter <very long search string> (200+ characters) in the Search field
3. Press Enter (or click the search action)
test_cases.expected_result: Either: (a) The Search field retains the full entered string and the Timeline refreshes showing matching results or the empty-state if none match (search executes successfully); OR (b) The UI prevents the query and shows an inline validation message adjacent to the Search field indicating the query is too long (search is blocked). The visible behavior (full text retained vs. validation message) is recorded by the tester.

test_cases.test_case: Search query with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed
preconditions: User is authenticated and Dashboard is open
Timeline block is visible
steps: 1. Click the Timeline Search field
2. Enter <search term> with leading and trailing whitespace (e.g., '  <term>  ') in the Search field
3. Press Enter (or click the search action)
test_cases.expected_result: The Timeline search executes using the trimmed query (leading/trailing whitespace removed). The Search field value shown after submission is the trimmed string (no leading/trailing spaces) and the Timeline refreshes to show matching results or the empty-state. This trim-and-search behavior succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate from December to January (year boundary) using Next Month
preconditions: User is authenticated and Dashboard is open
Calendar block is visible and its month heading currently shows 'December <year>'
steps: 1. Click the Next Month button in the Calendar block
test_cases.expected_result: Calendar updates to show 'January <year + 1>' in the month/year heading and event highlighting refreshes for the newly displayed month. The month→year rollover navigation succeeds (no UI overlap, heading shows the new year, and dates/events reflect the January view).

test_cases.test_case: Click Full calendar then immediately press browser Back — verify return state
preconditions: User is authenticated and Dashboard is open
Calendar block is visible
steps: 1. Click the 'Full calendar' link in the Calendar block
2. Immediately press the browser Back button
test_cases.expected_result: After pressing Back, the Dashboard is restored and the Calendar block is visible. The calendar heading matches the month that was shown on the Dashboard immediately before navigating away. The navigation to and back from the Full calendar view succeeds and does not leave the Dashboard in an inconsistent state.

test_cases.test_case: Reset page to default restores default layout
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
steps: 1. Click the 'Reset page to default' button in the top-right of the Dashboard Edit Mode
test_cases.expected_result: reverts layout to default for the user

test_cases.test_case: Open Add a block page from + Add a block button
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: opens Add_A_Block_Page

test_cases.test_case: Cancel from Add a block page returns to Dashboard without adding
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button to open the Add a block page
2. Click the 'Cancel' link at the bottom of the Add a block page
test_cases.expected_result: returns to the Dashboard without adding a block

test_cases.test_case: Move a block using the row-level Move icon
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
Dashboard contains multiple blocks so a reposition target is available
steps: 1. Click and hold the Move icon for <target block>
2. Drag <target block> to the desired new position within the Dashboard layout
3. Release to drop the block into the new position
test_cases.expected_result: initiates block repositioning; layout changes persisted for the user

test_cases.test_case: Open configuration controls for a block via its options menu
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
Dashboard has at least one block with an Options (three-dot) menu
steps: 1. Click the Options (three-dot) menu for <target block>
2. Click 'Configure' in the options menu
test_cases.expected_result: opens configuration controls for the selected block

test_cases.test_case: Move a block via the options menu Move action
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
Dashboard contains multiple blocks so a reposition target is available
steps: 1. Click the Options (three-dot) menu for <target block>
2. Click 'Move' in the options menu
3. Drag <target block> to the desired new position and drop it
test_cases.expected_result: initiates block repositioning; layout changes persisted for the user

test_cases.test_case: Delete a block via the options menu and confirm
preconditions: User logged in as <role>
User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).
Dashboard has at least one removable block
steps: 1. Click the Options (three-dot) menu for <target block>
2. Click 'Delete' in the options menu
3. Click Confirm on the Delete dialog
test_cases.expected_result: removes the block from the dashboard; layout changes persisted for the user

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user attempts to access Edit mode controls
preconditions: User is not authenticated
steps: 1. Open the application in a browser while not logged in
2. Navigate to the Dashboard page or the URL that would open Dashboard edit mode
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: the user is redirected to the login page; no Edit-mode controls are visible (no 'Reset page to default' button, no '+ Add a block' button, no block move icons or options menus) and no layout edit actions can be performed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to use 'Reset page to default' when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is authenticated
Dashboard Edit mode is toggled OFF
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user
2. Open the Dashboard page
3. Ensure Edit mode is toggled OFF
4. Look for the 'Reset page to default' button
test_cases.expected_result: The 'Reset page to default' button is not visible when Edit mode is OFF; the reset action cannot be triggered and the dashboard layout remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open 'Add a block' when Edit mode is OFF (button not present)
preconditions: User is authenticated
Dashboard Edit mode is toggled OFF
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user
2. Open the Dashboard page
3. Ensure Edit mode is toggled OFF
4. Look for the '+ Add a block' button below the Dashboard heading
test_cases.expected_result: The '+ Add a block' button is not visible when Edit mode is OFF; the Add a block page cannot be opened from the Dashboard and no block can be added

test_cases.test_case: Direct navigation to Add a block page is blocked when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is authenticated
Dashboard Edit mode is toggled OFF
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user
2. Open the Dashboard page
3. Ensure Edit mode is toggled OFF
4. Manually navigate to the Add a block page URL or attempt to open it via a direct link
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation to the Add a block page is blocked when Edit mode is OFF: the Add a block page is not displayed, the user remains on the standard Dashboard view, and no block addition UI or controls are available

test_cases.test_case: Options menu (Configure/Move/Delete) is not accessible when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is authenticated
Dashboard contains at least one block
Dashboard Edit mode is toggled OFF
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user
2. Open the Dashboard page that contains existing blocks
3. Ensure Edit mode is toggled OFF
4. Attempt to open a block's three-dot options menu (row-level options)
test_cases.expected_result: The block's options menu is not visible / does not open when Edit mode is OFF; the 'Configure', 'Move', and 'Delete' actions are not accessible and no block configuration, move, or delete can be performed

test_cases.test_case: Move icon/drag to reposition is disabled or absent when Edit mode is OFF
preconditions: User is authenticated
Dashboard contains at least one block
Dashboard Edit mode is toggled OFF
steps: 1. Log in as an authenticated user
2. Open the Dashboard page that contains existing blocks
3. Ensure Edit mode is toggled OFF
4. Attempt to click or drag a block using its Move icon
test_cases.expected_result: The Move icon is not visible or is disabled when Edit mode is OFF; dragging/repositioning does not start and the dashboard layout remains unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Toggle Edit mode off while a block move is in progress (move cancelled)
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Edit mode toggled on
At least one block is present on the Dashboard
steps: 1. Click the block's Move icon to initiate repositioning
2. While repositioning UI is active, click the Edit mode toggle to switch Edit mode off
test_cases.expected_result: The in-progress repositioning is cancelled and no layout change is persisted; the Dashboard returns to view mode and the Move controls (Move icon and options menu) are no longer visible — action is blocked (the attempted move does not succeed).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid consecutive Move attempts on the same block (second attempt ignored)
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Edit mode toggled on
At least two blocks are present so repositioning target exists
steps: 1. Click a block's Move icon to begin repositioning
2. Immediately (within a fraction of a second) click the Move icon again on the target position or re-click the original block's Move icon a second time
test_cases.expected_result: Only the first repositioning action is applied and persisted; the second rapid Move attempt is blocked and the UI shows no additional repositioning (no duplicate or conflicting moves are applied) — second attempt is blocked / error shown (or second click is ignored with a visible disabled state).

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back immediately after opening Add a block page (no block added)
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Edit mode toggled on
steps: 1. Click the '+ Add a block' button
2. Immediately press the browser Back button (before selecting any block type or interacting with the Add a block page)
test_cases.expected_result: The UI returns to the Dashboard and no new block is added; the Dashboard shows the same blocks it showed before opening the Add a block page — the add action does not succeed (no partial addition persisted).

test_cases.test_case: Delete all blocks then click 'Reset page to default' (empty state persisted, then default restored)
preconditions: User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Edit mode toggled on
At least one block is present on the Dashboard
steps: 1. For each block visible in Edit mode, open its Options menu
2. Click Delete in the Options menu for a block
3. Repeat steps 1–2 until no blocks remain on the Dashboard
4. Leave Edit mode (toggle Edit mode off) and observe the Dashboard
5. Re-enter Edit mode (toggle Edit mode on)
6. Click the 'Reset page to default' button
test_cases.expected_result: After step 3, the Dashboard shows no blocks and that empty layout persists when Edit mode is left and re-entered (deletion succeeds). After step 6, the Dashboard is reverted to the default layout and the default blocks reappear for the user — Reset page to default succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Open a course by clicking the course name link
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher> with access to one or more courses
steps: 1. Click 'My Courses' in the top navigation to open the My Courses page
2. Click the course name link for <target course> in the Courses grid
test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to Course Main Page

test_cases.test_case: Open a course by clicking the course card body
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher> with access to one or more courses
steps: 1. Click 'My Courses' in the top navigation to open the My Courses page
2. Click the body of the course card for <target course> (not the three-dot menu or course name link)
test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to Course Main Page

test_cases.test_case: Star a course from the card actions and verify it is pinned to the top
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher> with access to one or more courses
steps: 1. Click 'My Courses' in the top navigation to open the My Courses page
2. Open the three-dot menu on the card for <target course>
3. Click 'Star this course' in the card actions menu
test_cases.expected_result: pins the course to the top of the list

test_cases.test_case: Remove a course from view via card actions and verify it is hidden
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher> with access to one or more courses
steps: 1. Click 'My Courses' in the top navigation to open the My Courses page
2. Open the three-dot menu on the card for <target course>
3. Click 'Remove from view' in the card actions menu
test_cases.expected_result: hides the course from the user's view without affecting enrollment

test_cases.test_case: Filter, search and change layout to List view and verify filtered results and layout
preconditions: User logged in as <Teacher> with access to one or more courses
steps: 1. Click 'My Courses' in the top navigation to open the My Courses page
2. Select <Status> from the Status dropdown
3. Enter <search term> in the Search field and execute the search
4. Select <Sort option> from the Sort dropdown
5. Select 'List' from the Layout dropdown
test_cases.expected_result: Courses grid displays only courses matching <search term> and Status = <Status>; unrelated courses are no longer visible. The Courses view is presented in the List layout.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot access My Courses page
preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: 1. As an unauthenticated user (no session), navigate to <My Courses URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Navigation is blocked: user is redirected to the Login page; the Login form is visible and the Courses Grid is not displayed or accessible

test_cases.test_case: Logged-in user without any course access cannot view course cards
preconditions: User is logged in but has access to zero courses (no enrollments)
steps: 1. Log in as <user account with no course access>
2. Navigate to <My Courses URL>
test_cases.expected_result: Access precondition fails: the page shows an empty-state/access message and the Courses Grid is not populated; no course cards are shown and navigation to any Course Main Page is not possible from this view

test_cases.test_case: Search field accepts a very long query (>200 chars)
preconditions: User is logged in with access to one or more courses
My Courses page is open
steps: 1. Click the Search_Field
2. Type a very long string (greater than 200 characters) into the Search_Field
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search accepts the very long string and the search executes successfully; Courses_Grid updates (displays matching courses if any; otherwise shows the 'no results' / empty state). No UI error or crash is shown — operation succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Search with emoji, non-Latin unicode, and punctuation characters
preconditions: User is logged in with access to one or more courses
My Courses page is open
steps: 1. Click the Search_Field
2. Type a string containing emoji, non-Latin unicode characters, and punctuation (e.g., quotes, percent signs) into the Search_Field
3. Press Enter or click the search icon
test_cases.expected_result: Search accepts the special/unicode characters and executes successfully; Courses_Grid updates accordingly (shows matches or the 'no results' state). No input-related error is shown and the Search_Field preserves the entered characters — operation succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Remove from view then verify course appears under Status = Hidden
preconditions: User is logged in with access to one or more courses
At least one course is visible in the default Courses_Grid (Status = All)
steps: 1. Locate a visible course card in the Courses_Grid
2. Click the card's three-dot menu
3. Click the 'Remove from view' action
4. Observe the Courses_Grid immediately after the action
5. Open the Status_Filter dropdown
6. Select the 'Hidden' option in the Status_Filter
test_cases.expected_result: 'Remove from view' action succeeds: the course card is removed from the current (All) grid view (no longer visible). When Status_Filter is set to 'Hidden', the previously removed course appears in the Courses_Grid. The hide action does not affect enrollment and no error is shown — operation succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Navigate to a section using the Course Index sidebar
preconditions: User is logged in as <Enrolled student or teacher>
Course is available in <My Courses> or <Dashboard>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for <course> via My Courses or Dashboard
2. In the Course Index sidebar, click the <Index_Item_Link> for a section
test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to the selected section in the course content (Course Index sidebar link activates the section view). The selected section header is brought into view in the Course Content area and that section's activities and resources are visible.

test_cases.test_case: Toggle a single section to expand and show its activities and resources
preconditions: User is logged in as <Enrolled student or teacher>
Course is available in <My Courses> or <Dashboard>

2. Locate a section that is currently collapsed
3. Click the section's <Toggle_Collapse> control
test_cases.expected_result: The targeted section toggles between expanded and collapsed states; its activities and resources are shown or hidden accordingly. After clicking to expand, the section expands and its list of activities and resources becomes visible under the section header.

test_cases.test_case: Use 'Collapse all' to collapse every section so only headers remain

Course is available in <My Courses> or <Dashboard>

2. If necessary, expand two different sections by clicking their <Toggle_Collapse> controls so there are visible expanded sections
3. Click the 'Collapse all' link at the top right of the Course Content area
test_cases.expected_result: All course content sections collapse so only section headers remain visible.

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity from inside a section

Course is available in <My Courses> or <Dashboard>
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for <course> via My Courses or Dashboard

3. Click the <Activity_Link> for the target activity
test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to the selected activity (opens the activity page or view). The browser shows the selected activity's page/view (the activity header for <selected activity> is visible).

test_cases.test_case: Open a resource from inside a section

Course is available in <My Courses> or <Dashboard>

test_cases.expected_result: Navigates to or opens the selected resource. The resource's viewer or resource page is displayed and the resource title for <selected resource> is visible.

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot view Course Page (redirect to login)
preconditions: User is not logged in
steps: 1. Open the Course Page URL directly in a browser while not authenticated
2. Observe the resulting page
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the login page or shown a login prompt; the Course Page heading, Course Tab Bar, Course Index sidebar, and course sections are not displayed or accessible. The action is blocked: no course content is visible or interactable.

test_cases.test_case: Logged-in user who is not enrolled cannot access course content or use Course Index
preconditions: User is logged in but is not enrolled in the course / does not have access to the course
steps: 1. Log in as a user who is not enrolled in the course
2. Navigate to the Course Page via Dashboard or by selecting the course link
3. Attempt to use the Course Index sidebar link (Index_Item_Link) or interact with any section toggles
test_cases.expected_result: Page blocks access to course content: the course heading, Course Tab Bar, Course Index sidebar, section headers and their activities/resources are not shown or are disabled. An access-denied message is displayed (e.g., 'You do not have access to this course' or equivalent). The navigation action is blocked: the user is not taken to any section and cannot toggle or open section content.

test_cases.test_case: Rapid repeated toggling of a single section
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the course
Course contains at least one section with visible Activities and Resources
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for the course
2. Locate Section A in the Course_Content_Sections list
3. Click the Toggle_Collapse control for Section A
4. Immediately click the Toggle_Collapse control for Section A a second time
5. Immediately click the Toggle_Collapse control for Section A a third time
6. Observe the Section A header and its Activities and Resources list
test_cases.expected_result: After the three rapid Toggle_Collapse clicks, Section A is expanded (final state matches parity of clicks) and the Activities and Resources lists are visible; no duplicate activity/resource rows are rendered in the DOM and the UI remains responsive — action sequence succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Click 'Collapse all' when sections are partially expanded, then click it again immediately
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the course
Course contains multiple sections; at least two sections are expanded at test start
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for the course
2. Click the Toggle_Collapse control to ensure Section A is expanded
3. Click the Toggle_Collapse control to ensure Section B is expanded
4. Click the 'Collapse all' link in the Course_Content_Sections actions area
5. Immediately click the 'Collapse all' link again
test_cases.expected_result: The first 'Collapse all' click collapses all sections so only section headers remain visible; the immediate second click makes no visible change and produces no error message — 'Collapse all' action succeeds and is idempotent (sections remain collapsed).

test_cases.test_case: Use Course Index link to navigate to a section that is currently collapsed and offscreen
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the course
Course_Index_Sidebar is visible and includes a link to Section X
Section X is currently collapsed and located offscreen (requires scrolling)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for the course
2. Verify Section X header is collapsed and not in the current viewport
3. In the Course_Index_Sidebar, click the Index_Item_Link corresponding to Section X
test_cases.expected_result: The Course Page scrolls to Section X, the Section X header is expanded and its Activities and Resources lists become visible; focus/viewport moves to the expanded section — navigation via the index link succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Open an activity then immediately press the browser Back button
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the course
Course contains at least one section with at least one Activity_Link
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for the course
2. Expand the section containing Activity A so Activity_Link is visible
3. Click Activity_Link for Activity A
4. Wait until the Activity page/view has loaded
5. Press the browser Back button
test_cases.expected_result: After pressing Back, the Course Page is displayed; the section that contained Activity A is in a stable state (expanded or per the app's standard behavior) and the Activities list contains a single entry for Activity A (no duplicate entries were created). Navigation and history behavior succeeds (no error shown).

test_cases.test_case: Display behavior for a very long section name
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the course
Course contains a section whose name is a very long string (significantly longer than a single line)
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for the course
2. Observe the presentation of the very long section name in the section header
test_cases.expected_result: The very long section name is rendered without overlapping the chevron/toggle control; the title wraps to multiple lines (readable) and does not break layout or obscure the Toggle_Collapse control — display of the long name succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Activity and resource names containing emojis and special characters are displayed and actionable
preconditions: User is logged in and has access to the course
Course contains at least one activity and one resource whose display names include emoji and special characters
steps: 1. Navigate to the Course Page for the course
2. Expand the section(s) containing the activity and the resource
3. Observe the Activity_Link and Resource_Link text rendering
4. Click the Activity_Link that contains emoji/special characters
5. Return to the Course Page and click the Resource_Link that contains emoji/special characters
test_cases.expected_result: Activity and resource names render emoji and special characters in the Course Page link text without truncation or replacement; clicking each link opens the corresponding activity/resource view without error — display and navigation succeed.

Viewing an existing Course Page
steps: 1. While logged in as Student, navigate to the Course Page

test_cases.test_case: Activity Chooser: clicking Add with no tile selected is blocked

Course page is in Edit mode
Activity Chooser modal is open

2. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on any section to open the Activity Chooser modal
3. Do not select any activity/resource tile (no radio selected)

test_cases.test_case: Activity Chooser: filtered view with no visible tiles blocks Add
preconditions: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions
Viewing an existing Course Page
Course page is in Edit mode
Activity Chooser modal is open
steps: 1. Open the Activity Chooser modal from a Course Page in Edit mode
2. Set the Category Filter to a value that results in no visible activity tiles (e.g., choose a category that returns no results)
3. Verify no activity tiles are visible
4. Click the 'Add' button
test_cases.expected_result: Add action is blocked; Activity Chooser modal remains open; a visible inline message appears near the tiles or Add button indicating no activity is selected / no activities available in this filter (e.g. "No activities available / select an activity to add"); no activity creation form opens

test_cases.test_case: Bulk action cannot be performed when no activities are selected (toolbar hidden)

2. If there is a direct link/URL for Activity Chooser, attempt to navigate to that URL from the non-course page
test_cases.expected_result: Activity Chooser modal does not open from outside a Course Page; no '+ Add an activity or resource' control is present on non-course pages; navigating directly to the Activity Chooser URL is blocked or redirects to the Course listing or shows an access/context error banner indicating activities can only be added while viewing a Course Page

test_cases.test_case: Bulk actions toolbar appears when exactly one activity is selected
preconditions: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions and viewing an existing Course Page
Course page is in Edit mode
At least one section exists with one or more activity rows visible
steps: 1. Click the Select_For_Bulk checkbox for a single activity row
2. Observe the area where the Bulk_Actions_Toolbar should appear
test_cases.expected_result: Bulk_Actions_Toolbar becomes visible showing bulk action buttons (e.g., Delete Selected, Move Selected) and the buttons are enabled for use; this behavior succeeds (toolbar visible when exactly one item is selected).

test_cases.test_case: Bulk actions are not available when zero activities are selected (past-boundary)

Course page is in Edit mode
No Select_For_Bulk checkboxes are checked
steps: 1. Verify no Select_For_Bulk checkboxes are checked
2. Inspect the area where Bulk_Actions_Toolbar would appear and attempt to interact with a 'Delete Selected' control if present
test_cases.expected_result: Bulk_Actions_Toolbar remains hidden and there are no actionable bulk action controls; attempting to invoke a bulk action is blocked (no bulk action can be performed when zero items are selected) — the past-boundary is blocked / error shown (no bulk action performed).

test_cases.test_case: Rapid select/deselect race does not create duplicate bulk toolbars or leave stale toolbar
preconditions: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions and viewing an existing Course Page
Course page is in Edit mode
At least one section exists with one or more activity rows visible
steps: 1. Rapidly click the Select_For_Bulk checkbox on an activity row (select)
2. Within 300ms, click the same Select_For_Bulk checkbox again (deselect)
3. Observe the Bulk_Actions_Toolbar area for presence and count of toolbar instances
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate Bulk_Actions_Toolbar instances are created; after the quick deselect the toolbar remains hidden (no residual toolbar left behind); the UI state is consistent and this behavior succeeds (race does not produce duplicated or stuck toolbars).

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly switching selection between activity tiles results in a single final selection used by Add
preconditions: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions and viewing an existing Course Page
Course page is in Edit mode
Activity_Chooser_Modal opens when '+ Add an activity or resource' is clicked
steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' on any section to open Activity_Chooser_Modal (WF-007)
2. Click the 'Assignment' activity tile's Select control
3. Immediately click the 'Quiz' activity tile's Select control
4. Click the 'Add' button in the Activity_Chooser_Modal
test_cases.expected_result: Only the last-clicked tile ('Quiz') remains selected (radio semantics); clicking Add opens the Quiz creation form (WF-024) and only one creation form opens; this behavior succeeds (single final selection honored).

test_cases.test_case: Prevent duplicate activity creation when navigating back immediately after saving a newly added activity
preconditions: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions and viewing an existing Course Page
Course page is in Edit mode
A section is available to receive a new activity
steps: 1. Click '+ Add an activity or resource' to open Activity_Chooser_Modal (WF-007)
2. Select the 'Assignment' tile and click 'Add' to open the Assignment creation form (WF-022)
3. Fill the minimal required fields in the Assignment creation form (enter title or required inputs) — one atomic input per step
4. Click the form's Save/Finish button to create the Assignment
5. Immediately press the browser Back button once
test_cases.expected_result: No duplicate Assignment is created: only one new activity entry appears in the section after returning; any automatic re-submit or duplicate creation attempt is blocked (no second record is added) and the user sees the original single created activity in the section (duplicate creation is blocked).

test_cases.test_case: Leave required Assignment Name blank and attempt to save
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. Ensure the Assignment Creation form is open (precondition satisfied).
2. Leave the Assignment_Name field blank.
3. Click the 'Save and return to course' button.
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Assignment_Name field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; no assignment is created and the user remains on the creation form.

test_cases.test_case: Submit with all required fields empty
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. Ensure the Assignment Creation form is open (precondition satisfied).
2. Leave all required fields empty (representative: Assignment_Name blank).
3. Click the 'Save and display' button.
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Assignment_Name field indicating it is required; the form does not submit; no assignment is created and the user remains on the creation form.

test_cases.test_case: Enter non-numeric value into Max Number Of Uploaded Files when File Submissions enabled
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
Submission_Types -> File_Submissions checkbox is available
steps: 1. Ensure the Assignment Creation form is open (precondition satisfied).
2. Check the File_Submissions checkbox to reveal file-related controls.
3. Enter <non-numeric value> into the Max_Number_Of_Uploaded_Files field.
4. Click the 'Save and return to course' button.
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Max_Number_Of_Uploaded_Files field indicating it must be a numeric value; the form does not submit; no assignment is created.

test_cases.test_case: Enter invalid date into Due Date when Due Date enabled
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. Ensure the Assignment Creation form is open (precondition satisfied).
2. In the Availability panel, check the Due_Date Enabled checkbox to enable the Due Date picker.
3. Enter <invalid date format> into the Due_Date Date field.
4. Click the 'Save and display' button.
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Due_Date Date field indicating the date/time is invalid or incorrectly formatted; the form does not submit; no assignment is created.

test_cases.test_case: Open Add restriction picker and submit without selecting a restriction type
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. Ensure the Assignment Creation form is open (precondition satisfied).
2. Click the 'Add restriction' button to open the Restriction_Type_Picker.
3. Do not select any value in the Restriction_Type_Picker (leave it blank).
4. Click the 'Save and return to course' button.
test_cases.expected_result: Inline validation error appears on the Restriction_Type_Picker indicating a selection is required; the form does not submit; no assignment is created.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Assignment creation as a user without course editing permissions
preconditions: User DOES NOT have course editing permissions for the target course.
steps: 1. Log in as a user without course editing permissions.
2. Navigate to the course page.
3. Open the Activity Chooser.
4. Attempt to select the 'Assignment' activity (or navigate directly to the assignment creation URL).
test_cases.expected_result: The Assignment creation form is not displayed: the 'Assignment' option is not present in the Activity Chooser and direct navigation to the creation page is blocked; an authorization/error banner is visible indicating the user cannot add activities; no assignment creation UI is accessible.

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to open Assignment creation while Course Edit Mode is disabled
preconditions: User has course editing permissions but Course Edit Mode is disabled on the course page.
steps: 1. Log in as a user with course editing permissions.
2. Navigate to the course page and ensure Course Edit Mode is turned OFF.
3. Open the Activity Chooser.
4. Attempt to select the 'Assignment' activity.
test_cases.expected_result: The Assignment creation form does not open: the Activity Chooser does not present the 'Assignment' option while Edit Mode is disabled (or shows a message that editing must be enabled); no assignment creation UI is accessible and no save action is possible.

test_cases.test_case: Assignment name with leading and trailing whitespace is trimmed on save
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. From the Activity Chooser, select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form
2. Enter a name with leading and trailing spaces into the Assignment_Name field
3. Click the 'Save and display' button
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; assignment is created and the assignment detail page opens. The Assignment Name displayed on the assignment detail page has leading and trailing whitespace trimmed (no extra spaces visible).

test_cases.test_case: Very long Assignment name (>200 characters) behavior
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. From the Activity Chooser, select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form
2. Enter a very long string (greater than 200 characters) into the Assignment_Name field
3. Click the 'Save and display' button
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; assignment is created and the assignment detail page opens. The Assignment Name is preserved in the saved record; if the UI truncates the name, a visible truncation indicator (e.g., ellipsis with tooltip or 'truncated' marker) is shown on the detail page. In all cases the save operation succeeds.

test_cases.test_case: Rich text Description accepts emojis, special Unicode and neutralizes script-like input
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. From the Activity Chooser, select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form
2. In the Description rich text editor, paste content containing emoji, extended Unicode characters, and script-like tags
3. Click the 'Save and display' button
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; assignment is created and the assignment detail page opens. The Description displays emojis and special Unicode characters as entered. Any script-like tags are not executed; if sanitization removed or altered content, that change is visible on the detail page (no script execution occurs).

test_cases.test_case: Values entered into file-submission-specific fields are preserved when File_Submissions is toggled off before saving
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. From the Activity Chooser, select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form
2. Check the File_Submissions checkbox to enable file submissions
3. Enter a value into Max_Number_Of_Uploaded_Files
4. Enter a value into Max_Submission_Size
5. Uncheck the File_Submissions checkbox to disable file submissions
6. Click the 'Save and return to course' button
7. Reopen the created assignment's 'Edit settings' (Assignment Creation form) from the course page
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; assignment is created and the course page is shown. On reopening edit settings, File_Submissions is unchecked but the previously entered values for Max_Number_Of_Uploaded_Files and Max_Submission_Size remain populated (values are preserved). No validation error prevented the save.

test_cases.test_case: Browser Back + immediate resubmit after 'Save and display' does not create a duplicate assignment
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
File_Submissions == false, Group_Submissions == false, Add_Restriction_Button_Clicked == false (to match WF-002 branch)
steps: 1. From the Activity Chooser, select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form
2. Enter a unique value into the Assignment_Name field
3. Click the 'Save and display' button to create the assignment and open its page
4. Using the browser, click the Back button to return to the previous page (which may show the creation form)
5. Immediately click 'Save and display' again
test_cases.expected_result: Second submission is blocked / error shown: no duplicate assignment is created. Visible outcome: only one assignment appears in the course; either the creation form is blank or the UI shows an inline message indicating the assignment already exists or that the prior save completed, and a second creation does not occur.

test_cases.test_case: Setting Due Date to today's date/time with Allow_Submissions_From left disabled
preconditions: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.
steps: 1. From the Activity Chooser, select 'Assignment' to open the Assignment Creation form
2. In Availability > Due_Date group, check Enabled and set the Date/Time to today's date (current day)
3. In Availability > Allow_Submissions_From group, leave Enabled unchecked
4. Click the 'Save and display' button
test_cases.expected_result: Save succeeds; assignment is created and the assignment detail page opens. The Due Date displays exactly as entered (the same day) with no inline validation error about the date being invalid or in conflict with an unset Allow_Submissions_From (succeeds).

test_cases.test_case: Unauthenticated user cannot see or trigger Log out from the user menu

test_cases.test_case: Direct navigation to logout endpoint while not authenticated is blocked and redirects to login
preconditions: User is not authenticated (no active session); application opened in a fresh browser session
steps: 1. Start a fresh browser session where no user is logged in

3. Observe the resulting page
test_cases.expected_result: Browser is redirected to the Login page (login view is displayed); the application does not perform a logout operation because no session existed, and the user remains unauthenticated (no protected page is shown)

test_cases.test_case: Rapid double-click of Log out button
preconditions: User is authenticated and the user menu is available
steps: 1. Click the user initials to open the user menu
2. Rapidly double-click the "Log out" button (two clicks in quick succession)

test_cases.test_case: Press browser Back immediately after logout
preconditions: User is authenticated and the user menu is available
steps: 1. Click the user initials to open the user menu
2. Click the "Log out" button
3. After the app redirects to the login page, press the browser Back button
test_cases.expected_result: Back navigation is blocked; the login page remains visible (or the app immediately redirects back to the login page) and previously accessible protected page content is not presented. Any attempt to view protected pages requires re-authentication.

test_cases.test_case: Logout in one tab then refresh a protected page in another tab
preconditions: User is authenticated with two browser tabs open: Tab A (any app page) and Tab B (a protected page loaded)
steps: 1. In Tab A, click the user initials to open the user menu
2. In Tab A, click the "Log out" button
3. In Tab B, click the browser Refresh button while the user is logged out
test_cases.expected_result: Refresh is blocked: the protected page reload results in a redirect to the login page (user must re-authenticate); cached protected content is not left accessible in Tab B.

module_context.summary: Provides the authentication interface with username and password fields, options for guest access and cookie information, and inline error handling for failed sign-in attempts.
module_context.where_it_fits: Acts as the application's primary entry point for unauthenticated users before access to Dashboard, My Courses, and course pages; a successful teacher login redirects to the Dashboard referenced in the top navigation, while guest access permits limited unauthenticated browsing of site content.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is unauthenticated and at the site's login page (or choosing guest access); valid credentials are available for successful sign-in.

module_context.summary: Displays a personalized greeting and side-by-side Timeline and Calendar blocks summarizing upcoming teaching actions and month-view events.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the Dashboard link (alongside Home and My Courses), this module serves as the user’s personal overview/landing area after login and links into the dedicated calendar view and calendar data management pages. It sits before course-specific pages in the user flow, providing aggregated cross-course information that complements the Course Page and Course Index sidebar.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Authenticated (logged-in) user; user account exists and may have one or more enrolled courses (Timeline will be empty if none).

module_context.summary: Provides an interface for customizing the Dashboard layout by adding, configuring, moving, and removing blocks, with an option to reset the page to the default layout.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation via the Dashboard link, this mode is the Dashboard-specific editing state where block management controls (add block, move, configure, delete) and layout persistence are exposed; it complements the regular Dashboard viewing path and sits alongside other course/activity edit modes in the app's navigation structure.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is authenticated and viewing the Dashboard page, with Dashboard edit capability enabled (Edit mode toggled on).

module_context.summary: Displays all courses the user (typically a teacher) has access to as configurable visual cards with filtering, searching, sorting, and per-card actions.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the top navigation bar via the My Courses link (alongside Home and Dashboard), this page serves as the course-selection hub prior to entering a specific Course Page; selecting a course card navigates to that course's main page where the course tab bar and Course Index appear.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in user with access to one or more courses (e.g., a teacher)

module_context.summary: Displays the full course content organized into collapsible sections with activities and resources listed under each section.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the main navigation via Home / Dashboard / My Courses or by selecting a course link (breadcrumbs reflect the path), this view is the central course workspace where the horizontal course tab bar and Course Index sidebar provide navigation between course-level screens and activities.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and has access to the course (enrolled student or teacher) with the course available in My Courses or Dashboard.

module_context.module_title: Course Edit Mode and Activity Chooser
module_context.summary: Enables an authoring interface on a Course page with inline controls for editing, managing sections and activities, bulk actions, and an Activity Chooser modal for adding activities and resources.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is activated on a Course Page (reached via My Courses or Dashboard → select course) and switches the course view into edit mode for content creation and organization; it is primarily used by teachers during course setup and ongoing content edits (the Settings tab is visible to teachers).
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a user with editing/teacher permissions and currently viewing an existing Course Page.

module_context.summary: Form for configuring and creating an Assignment activity, including settings for availability, submission and feedback types, grading, completion, access restrictions, and notifications.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from the Course Edit Mode via the Activity Chooser when managing a course (reachable from My Courses or a Course Page); after saving it returns to the course page or opens the new assignment's page. It complements course-level navigation such as the Course tab and Course Index sidebar for adding activities to a course.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User with course editing permissions viewing a specific course with Course Edit Mode enabled and the Activity Chooser open.

module_context.summary: A collapsible form for viewing and editing a course's metadata, appearance, enrollment and participation settings, and completion/grouping options with inline validation and image/summary fields.
module_context.where_it_fits: This module is accessed from a course's horizontal tab bar under the Settings tab (visible to teachers) and sits within the course page workflow between Course Page and Course Edit Mode, persisting changes back to the course view (Save and display) or aborting (Cancel). It is reachable via the course breadcrumbs and the Course Index when a specific course is active.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: An active specific course is open and the user has teacher/manager permissions such that the Settings tab is visible.

module_context.summary: Lists and filters users enrolled in a course and provides per-user actions (view profile, edit role, message), bulk actions, and an enrolment dialog to add users with roles and durations.
module_context.where_it_fits: Opened from a course's horizontal tab bar via the Participants tab (reachable from Home > My Courses > [Course] > Participants), it sits alongside Course, Grades and Activities and is used during course administration to manage enrolled users. The module complements course navigation (breadcrumbs and Course Index sidebar) by focusing on user-level management within a specific course.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is logged in and viewing a specific course, with permission to view and manage participants (including enrolling users) when enrollment actions are required.

module_context.summary: Shows an individual assignment's metadata, grading summary metrics, description and attachments, and provides access to the per-student grading interface and assignment-related tabs.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessed from a course’s activity view (breadcrumbs and the course tab bar: Course / Activities) when viewing a specific assignment within a Course Page; it complements the Course Index sidebar and sits alongside tabs for Settings, Submissions, and Advanced grading in the assignment lifecycle.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a teacher and viewing a specific assignment activity within an existing course

module_context.summary: Displays a searchable and filterable table of all student submission records for an assignment, including submission and grading statuses, timestamps, previews, feedback, and quick-grading inline controls.
module_context.where_it_fits: Accessible from an assignment's activity page within a course (reachable via the Course tab and course breadcrumbs or the Course Index sidebar), this view is part of the teacher/grader workflow for reviewing and grading student work after submissions have been made. It sits downstream of Assignment Creation and is used prior to or during updating the Gradebook.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: User is viewing a specific course assignment and has grading permissions (e.g., teacher or grader), and at least the assignment exists (with one or more student submissions available).

module_context.summary: A spreadsheet-style Grader report that displays enrolled students as rows and course activities as columns, with per-column and per-cell controls, inline editing when Edit mode is enabled, and class averages shown.
module_context.where_it_fits: Located within a course's Grades tab in the horizontal course tab bar (Teachers see the Settings and Grades tabs), and selectable from the report-type dropdown when viewing grade reports; it complements other reports reachable from the user menu's Reports link and the course navigation. It is used during course assessment and grading workflows to view and edit grades for enrolled students.
module_context.assumed_state_on_entry: Logged in as a Teacher (role with grading privileges) viewing a specific course that has enrolled students and course activities, on the Grades tab with the Grader report available.
